---
title: "Day 1: Overview and Materials"
---

# Goals for Day 1

- **Familiarize yourself** with the basics of AI-assisted coding and its workflow.
- **Get an intuitive understanding** of the key concepts behind the technology,
  how it works and what are its capabilities, and limitations.
- **Setup the software environment** to be able to practice AI-assisted coding on
  your own during the self-study period following examples and
  supplementary materials.
- **See AI in action** by following along with live demonstrations and
  examples of common use cases, such as data preparation and cleaning, and
  securing sensitive data.

# Agenda

Day 1: Introduction and common workflows

| Time        | Topic |
|-------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| 9:00-9:10   | 1. [Welcome and course overview (slides)](#welcome) |
| 9:10-9:40   | 2. [Setup overview (slides)](#setup-overview). [Example 0 (live)](#example-0) and [Example 1 (self-study)](#example-1) |
| 9:40-10:20  | 3. [Example 2: Understanding data with AI (live)](#example-2) |
| 10:20-10:30 | ☕ Break (10 min) |
| 10:30-10:50 | 4. [Core AI-related concepts](#ai-concepts) |
| 10:50-11:00 | 5. [Common workflows and data safeguarding (slides)](#ai-workflow) |
| 11:00-11:30 | 6. [Example 3: Reproduce, Revise, and Reuse existing analysis (live/self-study)](#example-3) |

# Before You Arrive

(Try) [completing the prerequisites](/prerequisites/index.qmd).

If you run into issues, check [Common Problems](/prerequisites/common-problems.qmd) or
contact [ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org](mailto:ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org) before the session.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Day 1: Materials {#materials}


## 1. Learn: Welcome, introductions, and overview {#welcome}

Introduction and housekeeping, then an overview of the day's agenda and materials.

**Slides**

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck"  src="/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-1/index.html"></iframe>
```
View slides in a [new tab](/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-1/index.html){target="_blank"}.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Setup overview {#setup-overview}

> **Important**: Setup your working folders properly!

{{< include /day1/_working-folder.qmd >}}

**Slides**

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-2/index.html"></iframe>
```

View slides in a [new tab](/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-2/index.html){target="_blank"}.

### Example 0: Test your setup (live) {#example-0}

This presentation ends with an example that demonstrates how to test
if the setup is working correctly. Example 0 could be found in [Self-Study > Example 0](/selfstudy/example-0.qmd).

### Example 1: Stata in Positron with GitHub Copilot (self-study) {#example-1}

This introductory example is plan for self-study, full details, suggested
prompts, and instructions are in the [Self-Study > Example 1](/selfstudy/example-1.qmd).

{{< include /selfstudy/_ex1-1-overview.qmd >}}

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Example 2: Understanding data with AI (live) {#example-2}

This example is planned for live demonstration during the session, but you can
also follow along on your own as a self-study exercise. Full details, suggested
prompts, and instructions are in the [Self-Study > Example 2](/selfstudy/example-2.qmd).

### Overview

{{< include /selfstudy/_ex1-2-overview.qmd >}}

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Core AI-related concepts {#ai-concepts}

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-3/index.html"></iframe>

```

View slides in a [new tab](/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-3/index.html){target="_blank"}.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Common workflows and data safeguarding practices while using AI (slides) {#ai-workflow}


```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-4/index.html"></iframe>

```

View slides in a [new tab](/day1/ai4coding-slides-d1-4/index.html){target="_blank"}.


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. Example 3: Reproduce, Revise, and Reuse (live/self-study) {#example-3}

This example is planned for a live demonstration during the session, but you can
also follow along on your own as a self-study exercise. Full details, suggested
prompts, and instructions are in the [Self-Study > Example 3](/selfstudy/example-3.qmd).

Consider using your own data and problem for this example if you have one in
mind. The goal of this example is to practice the workflow of analysis
refactoring and improvement. The example provided is based on a real analysis,
but the workflow and principles apply broadly to any analysis you might be working on.

This example will show you how to use AI to:

1. Understand and reproduce existing analysis.
2. Revise it to make it clear, transparent, and well documented.
3. Set up system prompt files that guide the AI on dos and don'ts.
4. Adapt it to new data.
5. Document it in accordance with reproducibility package requirements.
